response:
the vlogger wants to create a schedule that maximizes the number of unique sequences of visiting these sites. the number of different sequences can be calculated by considering the permutations of 7 distinct items. the formula for permutations of n distinct items is n! (n factorial). for 7 sites, this is 7! = 5040 different sequences.

the vlogger also plans to spend a specific amount of time at each site. they estimate that the time spent at each site follows a normal distribution with a mean of 3 hours and a standard deviation of 0.5 hours. the probability that the total time spent visiting all 7 sites will exceed 22 hours can be calculated using the normal distribution. the mean total time is 7 * 3 = 21 hours, and the standard deviation is sqrt(7) * 0.5 ≈ 1.3229 hours. the z-score for 22 hours is (22 - 21) / 1.3229 ≈ 0.756. the probability that the total time exceeds 22 hours is the area under the normal curve to the right of z = 0.756, which is approximately 0.2257 or 22.57%.
